2026-07-20.patch_summary: This patch removes the legacy ->iomap_begin() and ->iomap_end() path in the iomap code, as all in-tree filesystems have implemented the new ->iomap_next() interface. The changes affect several files, including fs/iomap/direct-io.c and include/linux/iomap.h. The removal of this legacy path simplifies the code and reduces complexity.

reviews.author: changfengnan
reviews.summary: Reviewer changfengnan tested the patch on the simple path and found no performance degradation, but did not provide explicit approval or feedback.
reviews.raw_body:
Reviewed-by: Fengnan Chang <changfengnan@bytedance.com>

I ran some tests on the simple  path and didn't notice any
performance degradation.

2026-07-21.patch_summary: This patch removes the legacy ->iomap_begin() and ->iomap_end() path in the iomap code, as all in-tree filesystems have implemented the newer ->iomap_next() method. The changes affect several files, including fs/iomap/direct-io.c, fs/iomap/iter.c, and include/linux/iomap.h. The patch updates various functions to use the new ->iomap_next() method instead of the legacy path, simplifying the code and reducing complexity.
